note: NOMINAL contemporaneous price, split_factor 1 (PYPL has never split and pays no dividend, so the raw close already equals the contemporaneous nominal close). raw_close(last <= as_of) x 1 = $283.17, reconciling exactly to the card; validator refetched_yahoo 283.17 (delta 0.00%). Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the price feed: Q2-2021 10-Q share basis (~1,175M, S002) x card market cap $332.7B implies $283.17/sh (0.00%), plus dated 2021-07-28 reaction coverage (CNBC S004) framing the day-after close of $283.17 as a gap-down from $301.98. Separately, the card's cited as-of-era high ($310.16 intraday ~2021-07-26) matches the trailing-52wk nominal high computed here ($310.16) exactly (recorded in the 52wk_high field). Follows the NVDA exemplar pattern.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Not a hug: $283.17 is -8.7% off the trailing-52wk nominal high ($310.16) and +67.5% off the nominal low ($169.08) -- inside the band, beyond the 3% hug threshold on both sides. This is a post-earnings gap-down (from ~$301.98) the day after the Q2 print, not an all-time-high grab and not a 52wk-low grab, so no justification is required and T2 passes on band distance. The proximity to the high is the POINT of the expensive_quality_compounder_derating_risk case (near-ATH, ~60x fwd), but at -8.7% it is not a mechanical hug.

live_fields.value: As of 2021-07-29: Q3'21 net-revenue guidance ~$6.15-6.25B (+13-14% at spot), a STEP-DOWN from Q2's +19%, and FY21 non-GAAP EPS guide ~$4.70 (the ~60x-forward anchor). NNAs were 11.4M vs 21.3M a year earlier (-47%). This decelerating guide against a near-ATH price is the binding constraint. Do NOT use actual Q3'21 results or any post-as_of guidance.

sources.note: 8-K Ex-99.1: Q3'21 revenue guide ~$6.15-6.25B (+13-14%); FY21 non-GAAP EPS ~$4.70; NNAs 11.4M
as_of reaction: beat/miss vs Refinitiv, after-hours drop on guidance + eBay drag

live_fields.value: As of 2021-07-29: PYPL trades ~60x FY21 non-GAAP EPS (~$4.70) / ~67-80x trailing GAAP at a near-all-time-high (~$306-310 era, ~$310.16 intraday 2021-07-26), while growth is VISIBLY decelerating (NNAs -47% YoY; Q3 rev guide ~13-14% vs Q2 +19%) and trailing EPS is flattered by a ~$0.11 credit-reserve release ($0.20 GAAP portfolio gain), with eBay TPV rolling off (4% from 9%, ~done by Q3'21) and intensifying competition (Square/Cash App Q1'21 GP +79%/+171%, Apple Pay, BNPL). This is the as_of de-rating setup -- a permanent-loss-of-RETURN (overpay) risk, not solvency. NOTHING about the subsequent multiple compression is used (post as_of).

sources.note: NNAs -47%, Q3 guide +13-14%, ~$0.11 reserve release, eBay roll-off, non-GAAP op margin -171bps -> growth-quality erosion
Feb-2021 2025 aspirations (750M accounts, >$50B rev, ~$2.8T TPV) the ~60x multiple capitalizes
dated competitive landscape (Affirm/Afterpay/Klarna/Apple Pay) pressuring take-rate

live_fields.value: As of 2021-07-29: PayPal pays NO dividend (it had never paid one as a standalone public company); capital is returned via buybacks and deployed into acquisitions (Honey ~$4B / ~40x, a flagged capital-allocation concern). So unlike PFE/DIS there is no dividend signal in this case -- the entire return depends on growth and multiple, which sharpens the overpay/de-rating risk.

sources.note: no declared common dividend; net-cash balance sheet; buyback + M&A capital allocation
